currentSupportedBoundary.supportedFixture: Todo Search whitespace-normalization bug_fix dry-run fixture
currentSupportedBoundary.humanDecisionScope: current Todo Search fixture, current generated candidate, and current promotion review packet only

changeType.reason: The draft is recognized as behavior-change shaped for calibration policy only while preserving current bug_fix support boundaries.

evidenceShape.reason: The draft uses external project test, dogfood report, graph delta, and graph update proposal evidence rather than the Todo Search runtime/read-model evidence chain.

riskShape.reason: The draft now has behavior-change risk vocabulary for runtime compatibility, input coercion, Symbol handling, escaping correctness, non-string input drift, test coverage, and source-authority boundary risks, but no mitigation is proven.

scopeShape.reason: The draft scope includes external checkout paths under work/external/escape-html. Static metadata identifies the path as component/escape-html calibration context, but it remains non-portable and not compiler support authority.

contextShape.reason: The graph snapshot and change record now expose approximate source/test anchors for Symbol stringification, but exact line ranges are not verified and anchors are not supported compiler context.

validationCheckMapping.reason: External checks such as check-escape-html-npm-test and check-escape-html-dogfood-validator are previewed against observed dogfood Evidence, but they remain outside the current compiler required-check registry and await authoritative, portable checkout modeling.

outputRequirementSourceShape.reason: The existing output requirement source model can describe changed-file, command-output, validation-summary, and boundary obligations, but graph delta changed-file bindings are review artifacts rather than actual git diff authority.

unsupportedReasons.reason: The supported compiler command still reads only the Todo Search dry-run input path.
The current human decision is scoped to the Todo Search fixture and does not approve this calibration fixture.
The source dogfood is behavior-change shaped and recognized as a calibration draft, but this does not add supported behavior-change compilation.
The draft check IDs for npm test, dogfood validation, and graph-delta review now have preview bindings, but those previews are not current compiler required-check IDs or CI gates.
Allowed scope points at a local external checkout under work/external/escape-html. The path is now previewed as calibration-local context, not a committed repo path or supported compiler source.

blockedReasonsIfWired.reason: External check binding and checkout authority are previewed, but current required-check generation cannot treat them as authoritative or supported.
Current source-authority validation expects committed fixture paths for compiler input cross references; work/external/escape-html remains calibration-local and non-portable.
The draft now has preview risk vocabulary, but those terms are not final compiler risk policy and do not prove mitigation.

sourceAuthorityReuse.reason: Changed files, command Evidence, validation summary, and boundary statements fit the existing source model, but graph delta changed files are review artifacts and not execution authority.
Policy-derived forbidden scope maps well to no metadata, no upstream approval, and no graph apply boundaries; external checkout paths are now previewed but not supported.
Missing checkout, missing test command, and scope expansion map to existing stop source concepts, but the external check IDs are unsupported.
The fixture can map external_project_test_result, dogfood_report, and graph_delta_report to preview check bindings, but those bindings remain non-authoritative and outside compiler support.
Graph artifacts and change records now identify source/test behavior anchors, but line ranges and portable source authority remain unresolved.
Behavior-change risk terms now preserve stringification, escaping, test coverage, and source-authority boundary detail, but the preview does not prove mitigation or support.
Allowed code/test paths are identified as component/escape-html calibration-local checkout paths, but remain non-portable and not supported compiler source paths.

sourceAuthorityGaps.recommendedFollowUp: Use the previewed binding as calibration evidence only; do not add supported required-check registry entries without separate approval.
Treat work/external/escape-html as calibration-local source context only; do not use it as execution or compiler support authority.
Use preview risk vocabulary as calibration review metadata only; do not treat it as mitigation proof or final compiler risk policy.
Use anchor-level context as calibration review metadata only; do not treat anchors as edit permission or supported compiler context.
Keep graph delta and graph update proposal bindings review-only and distinct from graph apply authority.

recommendedNextStep.summary: Decide whether graph-delta review binding should be the next v0.3 calibration scope.
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Behavior-change policy, external required-check binding, checkout path authority, anchor-level context, and risk vocabulary now have preview boundaries, while the fixture remains not supported and non-enforcing.

promotionEligibility.reason: The second fixture is calibration-only and has source-authority gaps. It has no promotion review packet or human decision.

nonExecutionBoundary: This observation is non-enforcing preview Evidence only. It does not mark the second fixture as supported, compile a candidate, execute AI, apply graph deltas, approve promotion, accept work, enable required checks, configure branch protection, or retire tree-native artifacts.
